# UC-12.7.14 · Control-M Calendar-Based Scheduling Conflict Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Risk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch batch and control jobs so missed handoffs or resource limits do not break business processing.*

---

## Description

Shared calendars coordinate holidays and fiscal periods; conflicting edits from multiple operators can create impossible schedules or duplicate firings. Auditing calendar mutations alongside job cardinality exposes risky overlap before production dates misfire.

## Value

Reduces preventable missed batches around holidays and fiscal close when calendars are edited under pressure.

## Implementation

Align `calendar_name` field between audit and job feeds. If join cardinality is high, replace with `lookup calendars_jobs.csv`. Alert on `DELETE` actions touching calendars referenced by finance-tier jobs.

## SPL

```spl
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:audit"
| search object_type IN ("CALENDAR","ORDER","JOB") AND match(lower(action),"(?i)create|update|delete")
| eval cal_key=coalesce(calendar_name, object_name)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(user) as editors, count as changes, values(action) as actions by cal_key, _time
| where changes >= 5 AND editors >= 2
| sort -changes
```

## Visualization

Network diagram (calendar → jobs), change timeline, table of editors vs impacted job counts.

## Known False Positives

Transient errors during platform maintenance, network blips, or certificate renewals on shared services.

## References

- [BMC Control-M Calendars](https://documents.bmc.com/supportu/controlm/)
